name: Da Nang
description: Vietnam's most livable city punches far above its weight — world-class beaches, the ancient trading town of Hoi An 30 minutes away, Ba Na Hills' hilltop French village, and seafood so fresh it almost walks off the grill.

quickInfo.visa: E-visa 90 days, multiple entry. Free for some nationalities.

hotels.name: Memory Hostel Da Nang
hotels.description: Friendly beach hostel steps from My Khe, great breakfast and helpful staff

hotels.name: Novotel Da Nang Premier Han River
hotels.description: Stylish riverside hotel with infinity pool and stunning views of the Dragon Bridge

hotels.name: Fusion Maia Da Nang
hotels.description: All-spa-inclusive luxury resort where every villa includes unlimited spa treatments daily

hotels.name: Hyatt Regency Da Nang
hotels.description: Beachfront luxury resort with five pools cascading to the white sand of Non Nuoc Beach

hotels.name: Danang Marriott Resort & Spa
hotels.description: Lush peninsula resort surrounded by jungle, near Monkey Mountain, stunning bay views

activities.name: Hoi An Ancient Town Full-Day Tour
activities.description: Explore the UNESCO ancient trading town: Japanese Bridge, tailors, lantern shops, river cruise

activities.name: Ba Na Hills & Golden Bridge Day Trip
activities.description: Longest cable car in the world to the hilltop French village + the famous Giant Hands Bridge

activities.name: Da Nang Dragon Bridge Fire Show & City Night Tour
activities.description: Witness the Dragon Bridge breathe fire (Sat/Sun nights) with a local night food tour

activities.name: My Son Sanctuary UNESCO Ruins Tour
activities.description: Visit 4th-century Cham Hindu temple ruins hidden in a jungle valley — Vietnam's Angkor

activities.name: Marble Mountains & Da Nang Cooking Class
activities.description: Explore ancient Buddhist caves and marble workshops, then cook 4 Central Vietnamese dishes

items.activity: My Khe Beach morning swim (Vietnam's best city beach)
Marble Mountains cave temples & panoramic views
My Khe seafood lunch: grilled clams & fresh fish
Han Market & Dragon Bridge area stroll
Son Tra Peninsula: Lady Buddha statue viewpoint
Bus or taxi to Hoi An (30 min)
Ancient town: Japanese Covered Bridge & old merchant houses
White Rose dumplings & cao lau noodles lunch
Bicycle to An Bang Beach (5 km from town)
Lantern-lit Hoi An evening & boat ride

3days.title: Ba Na Hills & My Son Ruins

items.activity: Ba Na Hills cable car & Golden Bridge
Ba Na Hills French Village lunch
Check out pick-up tailor order from Hoi An
Farewell dinner: banh xeo sizzling crepe at local restaurant

practicalInfo.gettingThere: Da Nang International Airport (DAD) — Vietnam's 3rd busiest. Direct flights from Singapore, Bangkok, Seoul, Tokyo, Taipei, Hong Kong, Kuala Lumpur. Taxi to city: 15 min, $5. Airport near the city center.
practicalInfo.localTransport: Grab app excellent. Motorbike rental $6-8/day recommended for My Khe beach strip. Taxi metered (Mai Linh/Vinasun). Bicycle rental from Hoi An guesthouses $3/day. No real public transport.
